title: Graph interpretation word problems
description: Match features of graphs of modeling functions to their real-world meaning.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sabin opened a new martial arts gym.

$N(t)$ models the net worth of the gym (in thousands of dollars) after $t$ months.

**Match each statement with the feature of the graph that most closely corresponds to it.**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
A relative maximum (or minimum) point is a point that is higher (or lower) than all of the points surrounding it.

This graph has a relative minimum point at $t=6$, which means that *the gym's net worth was at its lowest at $\textit{6}$ months.*



[[☃ image 1]]

A positive (or negative) interval is a domain interval over which the function values are all positive (or negative).

Since $N(t)<0$ over the interval $[2,10]$, this is a negative interval. This means that *the gym's losses exceeded its profits between $\textit{2}$ and $\textit{10}$ months after opening.*



[[☃ image 1]]

An increasing (or decreasing) interval is a domain interval over which the function values increase (or decrease) as the input variable increases.

In this graph, the interval $[0,6]$ is a decreasing interval. This means that *the gym was losing money for the first $\textit{6}$ months.*



[[☃ image 1]]

To sum it up, the table should look as follows:

Feature | Statement
:-: | :-:
Relative maximum or minimum | The gym's net worth was at its lowest at $6$ months.
Positive or negative interval | The gym's losses exceeded its profits between $2$ and $10$ months after opening.
Increasing or decreasing interval | The gym was losing money for the first $6$ months.

options.alt: The first and fourth quadrants of a coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by one and is labeled t for the number of months. The y-axis scales by one and is labeled N of t for the net worth of the gym in thousands of dollars. The graph of the function is a continuous curve. From left to right, it starts at the y-intercept zero, four, then it decreases through the x-intercept zero, two and the point four, negative two point five until it reaches a local minimum at six, negative three. Then it increases through the point eight, negative two point five, the x-intercept ten, zero, and the point twelve, four.

right: The gym's net worth was at its lowest at $6$ months.
The gym's losses exceeded its profits between $2$ and $10$ months after opening.
The gym was losing money for the first $6$ months.

assessment_items.question_content:
Archimedes is making waves as he showers in his bath.

$H(t)$ models the height (in centimeters) of the water as a function of time (in seconds).

**Match each statement with the feature of the graph that most closely corresponds to it.**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
The $y$-intercept is the point of intersection between the graph of the function $H(t)$ and the $y$-axis, which is the point on the graph where $t=0$. 

The $y$-intercept in this graph is $(0,30)$, which means that *at the beginning, the surface's height was $\textit{30}$ centimeters.*


[[☃ image 1]]

A relative maximum (or minimum) point is a point that is higher (or lower) than all of the points surrounding it.

This graph has a relative maximum point at $t=6$, which means that *the water was at a crest after $\textit{6}$ seconds.*



[[☃ image 1]]

An increasing (or decreasing) interval is a domain interval over which the function values increase (or decrease) as the input variable increases.

In this graph, the interval $[12,15]$ is a decreasing interval. This means that *the surface descended between $\textit{12}$ and $\textit{15}$ seconds.*



[[☃ image 1]]

To sum it up, the table should look as follows:

Feature | Statement
:-: | :-:
$y$-intercept | At the beginning, the surface's height was $30$ centimeters.
Relative maximum or minimum | The water was at a crest after $6$ seconds.
Increasing or decreasing interval | The surface descended between $12$ and $15$ seconds.

options.alt: The first quadrant of a coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by two and is labeled t for the number of seconds. The y-axis scales by four and is labeled H of t for the height of the water. The graph of the function is a continuous curve. From left to right, it starts at the y-intercept zero, thirty, then it decreases through the point two, twenty-two until a local minimum at three, eighteen. Then it increases through the point four, twenty-two and the point five, twenty-eight until a local maximum at six, thirty-two. Then it decreases through the point seven, twenty-eight and the point eight, twenty-two until the local maximum at nine, twenty. Then it increases through the point ten, twenty-two and the point eleven, twenty-eight until the local maximum at twelve, thirty-four. Then it decreases through the point thirteen, twenty-eight and fourteen, twenty until a local minimum at fifteen, sixteen. Then it increases through the point sixteen, twenty and the point seventeen, twenty-eight until a local maximum at eighteen, thirty. Then it decreases through the point nineteen, twenty-seven.

right: At the beginning, the surface's height was $30$ centimeters.
The water was at a crest after $6$ seconds.
The surface descended between $12$ and $15$ seconds.

assessment_items.question_content:
$T(t)$ models the temperature (in degrees Celsius) in New York City when it's $t$ hours after midnight on a given day.

**Match each statement with the feature of the graph that most closely corresponds to it.**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
The $y$-intercept is the point of intersection between the graph of the function $T(t)$ and the $y$-axis, which is the point on the graph where $t=0$. 

The $y$-intercept in this graph is $(0,-3)$, which means that *it was $\textit{-3}\,^\circ\textit{C}$ at the beginning of the day.*



[[☃ image 1]]

A positive (or negative) interval is a domain interval over which the function values are all positive (or negative).

Since $T(t)>0$ over the interval $[8,20]$, this is a positive interval. This means that *the temperature was above zero between $\textit{8 am}$ and $\textit{8 pm}$.*



[[☃ image 1]]

An increasing (or decreasing) interval is a domain interval over which the function values increase (or decrease) as the input variable increases.

In this graph, the interval $[2,14]$ is an increasing interval. This means that *it was getting warmer between $\textit{2 am}$ and $\textit{2 pm}$.*



[[☃ image 1]]

To sum it up, the table should look as follows:

Feature | Statement
:-: | :-:
$y$-intercept | It was $-3^\circ\text{C}$ at the beginning of the day.
Positive or negative interval | The temperature was above zero between $8\text{ am}$ and $8\text{ pm}$.
Increasing or decreasing interval | It was getting warmer between $2\text{ am}$ and $2\text{ pm}$.

options.alt: The first and fourth quadrants of a coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by one and is labeled t for the number of hours after midnight. The y-axis scales by zero point five and is labeled T of t for the temperature, in degrees Celsius, of New York City. The graph of the function is a continuous curve. From left to right, it starts at the y-intercept zero, negative three. Then it decreases to the a local minimum at two, negative three point five. It increases through the point five, negative two point five, the x-intercept eight, zero, and the point eleven, one point seven-five until a local maximum at fourteen, two point five. Then it decreases though the point eighteen, one point two-five and the x-intercept twenty, zero.

right: It was $-3^\circ\text{C}$ at the beginning of the day.
The temperature was above zero between $8\text{ am}$ and $8\text{ pm}$.
It was getting warmer between $2\text{ am}$ and $2\text{ pm}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Keith is an elevator operator in a big hotel.

$A(t)$ models Keith's altitude (in meters relative to the ground) as a function of time (in minutes).

**Match each statement with the feature of the graph that most closely corresponds to it.**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
A relative maximum (or minimum) point is a point that is higher (or lower) than all of the points surrounding it.

This graph has a relative minimum point where $A(t)=-12$, which means that *Keith's lowest altitude was $\textit{12}$ meters below the ground.*



[[☃ image 1]]

A positive (or negative) interval is a domain interval over which the function values are all positive (or negative).

Since $A(t)>0$ over the interval $[6.5,10]$, this is a positive interval. This means that *between the $\textit{6.5}$- and $\textit{10}$-minute marks, Keith was above the ground.*



[[☃ image 1]]

An increasing (or decreasing) interval is a domain interval over which the function values increase (or decrease) as the input variable increases.

In this graph, the interval $[5.5,8]$ is an increasing interval. This means that *between the $\textit{5}$- and $\textit{8}$-minute marks, the elevator was going up.*



[[☃ image 1]]

To sum it up, the table should look as follows:

Feature | Statement
:-: | :-:
Relative maximum or minimum | Keith's lowest altitude was $12$ meters below the ground.
Positive or negative interval | Between the $6.5$- and $10$-minute marks, Keith was above the ground.
Increasing or decreasing interval | Between the $5$- and $8$-minute marks, the elevator was going up.

options.alt: The first and fourth quadrants of a coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by zero point five and is labeled t for the number of minutes. The y-axis scales by two and is labeled T of t for Keith's altitude in meters relative to the ground. The graph of the function is made up of line segments. From left to right, it starts at the y-intercept zero, eight. A line segment connects zero, eight to one, fourteen. A line segment connects one, fourteen to two, fourteen. A line segment connects two, fourteen to three, zero. A line segment connects three, zero to three point five, zero. A line segment connects three point five, zero to five, negative twelve. A line segment connects five, negative twelve to six point five, zero. A line segment connects six point five, zero to eight, twenty. A line segment connects eight, twenty to eight point five, fourteen. A line segment connects eight point five, fourteen to nine, fourteen. A line segment connects nine, fourteen to ten, zero.

right: Keith's lowest altitude was $12$ meters below the ground.
Between the $6.5$- and $10$-minute marks, Keith was above the ground.
Between the $5$- and $8$-minute marks, the elevator was going up.

assessment_items.question_content:
Dina discovered a water leak in her basement and called a plumber to fix and drain it.

$W(t)$ models the amount of water in the basement (in liters) $t$ hours since the leak began.

**Match each statement with the feature of the graph that most closely corresponds to it.**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
The $x$-intercept is the point of intersection between the graph of the function $W(t)$ and the $x$-axis, which is the point on the graph where $W(t)=0$. In this case the horizontal axis represents the variable $t$, but it's common to call that axis the $x$-axis.

This graph has an $x$-intercept at $(9,0)$, which means that *the plumber drained all the water out after $\textit{9}$ hours.*



[[☃ image 1]]

A relative maximum (or minimum) point is a point that is higher (or lower) than all of the points surrounding it.

This graph has a relative maximum point where $W(t)=70$, which means that *the amount of water reached $\textit{70}$ liters before the draining began.*



[[☃ image 1]]

An increasing (or decreasing) interval is a domain interval over which the function values increase (or decrease) as the input variable increases.

In this graph, the interval $[0,5]$ is an increasing interval. This means that *over the first $\textit{5}$ hours, the leak just got bigger and bigger.*



[[☃ image 1]]

To sum it up, the table should look as follows:

Feature | Statement
:-: | :-:
$x$-intercept | The plumber drained all the water out after $9$ hours.
Relative maximum or minimum | The amount of water reached $70$ liters before the draining began.
Increasing or decreasing interval | Over the first $5$ hours, the leak just got bigger and bigger.

options.alt: The first quadrant of a coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by zero point five and is labeled t for the number of hours since the leak began. The y-axis scales by five and is labeled  W of t for the amount of water in the basement, in liters. The graph of the function is made up of a line segment and a curve. From left to right, it starts at the y-intercept zero, zero. A line segment connects zero, zero to five, seventy. Then the function decreases at a non linear rate through the point six point five, sixty-five, the point seven point five, fifty-six, and the point eight point five, twenty-five until stopping at the x-intercept nine, zero.

right: The plumber drained all the water out after $9$ hours.
The amount of water reached $70$ liters before the draining began.
Over the first $5$ hours, the leak just got bigger and bigger.

assessment_items.question_content:
The altitude of a tree affects the rate at which the tree grows.

$G(A)$ models the growth rate (in millimeters per year) of a certain kind of tree at an altitude $A$ (in kilometers above sea level).

**Match each statement with the feature of the graph that most closely corresponds to it.**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
The $y$-intercept is the point of intersection between the graph of the function $G(A)$ and the $y$-axis, which is the point on the graph where $A=0$. 

The $y$-intercept in this graph is $(0,1.4)$, which means that *at sea level, the tree grows by $\textit{1.4}$ millimeters each year.*



[[☃ image 1]]

A relative maximum (or minimum) point is a point that is higher (or lower) than all of the points surrounding it.

This graph has a relative maximum point where $G(A)=2.6$, which means that *the fastest possible growth rate is $\textit{2.6}$ millimeters per year.*



[[☃ image 1]]

An increasing (or decreasing) interval is a domain interval over which the function values increase (or decrease) as the input variable increases.

In this graph, the interval $[7,14]$ is a decreasing interval. This means that *as the altitude surpasses $\textit{7}$ kilometers above sea level, the growth rate of the tree declines.*



[[☃ image 1]]

To sum it up, the table should look as follows:

Feature | Statement
:-: | :-:
$y$-intercept | At sea level, the tree grows by $1.4$ millimeters each year.
Relative maximum or minimum | The fastest possible growth rate is $2.6$ millimeters per year.
Increasing or decreasing interval | As the altitude surpasses $7$ kilometers above sea level, the growth rate of the tree declines.

options.alt: The first quadrant of a coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by zero point five and is labeled A for the altitude which is measured in kilometers above sea level. The y-axis scales by zero point one and is labeled G of A for the growth rate of the tree. The graph of the function is a continuous curve. From left to right, it starts at the y-intercept zero, one point four. It increases through the point two, two and the point five, two point five until it reach a local maximum at seven, two point six. Then it decreases through the point nine, two point five, the point twelve, two, and the point fourteen, one point four.

right: At sea level, the tree grows by $1.4$ millimeters each year.
The fastest possible growth rate is $2.6$ millimeters per year.
As the altitude surpasses $7$ kilometers above sea level, the growth rate of the tree declines.

assessment_items.question_content:
The efficiency of a motor can be measured by the percentage of the input power that the motor uses.

$E(c)$ models the efficiency (in percentage points) of a certain motor as a function of the power input's current $c$ (in amperes).

**Match each statement with the feature of the graph that most closely corresponds to it.**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
The $x$-intercept is the point of intersection between the graph of the function $E(c)$ and the $x$-axis, which is the point on the graph where $E(c)=0$. In this case the horizontal axis represents the variable $c$, but it's common to call that axis the $x$-axis.

This graph has an $x$-intercept at $(3,0)$, which means that *the motor loses all efficiency when the current hits $\textit{3}$ amperes.*



[[☃ image 1]]

A relative maximum (or minimum) point is a point that is higher (or lower) than all of the points surrounding it.

This graph has a relative maximum point where $E(c)\approx 50$, which means that *at its most efficient, the motor uses about $\textit{50%}$ of the input power.*



[[☃ image 1]]

An increasing (or decreasing) interval is a domain interval over which the function values increase (or decrease) as the input variable increases.

In this graph, the interval $[1,3]$ is a decreasing interval. This means that *as the input power's current grows beyond $\textit 1$ ampere, the motor becomes less efficient.*



[[☃ image 1]]

To sum it up, the table should look as follows:

Feature | Statement
:-: | :-:
$x$-intercept | The motor loses all efficiency when the current hits $3$ amperes.
Relative maximum or minimum | At its most efficient, the motor uses about $50\%$ of the input power.
Increasing or decreasing interval | As the input power's current grows beyond $1$ ampere, the motor becomes less efficient.

options.alt: The first quadrant of a coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by zero point two-five and is labeled c for the power input's current in amperes. The y-axis scales by five and is labeled E of c for the efficiency in percentage points of a motor. The graph of the function is a continuous curve. From left to right, it starts at the x-intercept zero point four, zero and increases through the point zero point five, thirty and the point zero point seven-five, forty-eight until a local maximum at zero point nine, fifty where it stalls slightly. The function decreases from the point one, fifty through the point two, thirty to the x-intercept three, zero. All values are estimates.

right: The motor loses all efficiency when the current hits $3$ amperes.
At its most efficient, the motor uses about $50\%$ of the input power.
As the input power's current grows beyond $1$ ampere, the motor becomes less efficient.

assessment_items.question_content:
A car's fuel efficiency is measured by the distance it can drive per a single unit of fuel. A common unit for efficiency is $\text{km/l}$, kilometers per liter of fuel.

$C(S)$ models the fuel efficiency of a certain car as a function of the car's speed $S$ (in kilometers per hour).

**Match each statement with the feature of the graph that most closely corresponds to it.**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
The $y$-intercept is the point of intersection between the graph of the function $C(S)$ and the $y$-axis, which is the point on the graph where $S=0$. 

The $y$-intercept in this graph is $(0,0)$, which means that *when the car doesn't move, it doesn't consume any fuel.*


[[☃ image 1]]

A relative maximum (or minimum) point is a point that is higher (or lower) than all of the points surrounding it.

This graph has a relative maximum point where $C(S)=20$, which means that *the most efficient the car can get is $\textit{20 km/l}$.*



[[☃ image 1]]

An increasing (or decreasing) interval is a domain interval over which the function values increase (or decrease) as the input variable increases.

In this graph, the interval $[0,55]$ is an increasing interval. This means that *as the car accelerates towards about $\textit{55 km/h}$, it gets more efficient.*



[[☃ image 1]]

To sum it up, the table should look as follows:

Feature | Statement
:-: | :-:
$y$-intercept | When the car doesn't move, it doesn't consume any fuel.
Relative maximum or minimum | The most efficient the car can get is $20\text{ km/l}$.
Increasing or decreasing interval | As the car accelerates towards about $55\text{ km/h}$, it gets more efficient.

options.alt: The first quadrant of a coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by five and is labeled S for the car's speed in kilometers per hour. The y-axis scales by one and is labeled C of S for the fuel efficiency of the car. The graph of the function is a continuous curve. From left to right, it starts at zero, zero and increases through the point ten, seven, the point twenty, thirteen, and the point forty, nineteen until it reaches a local maximum at fifty-five, twenty. Then it decreases through the point seventy, nineteen, the point ninety, sixteen, and the point one hundred twenty, twelve. All values are estimates.

right: When the car doesn't move, it doesn't consume any fuel.
The most efficient the car can get is $20\text{ km/l}$.
As the car accelerates towards about $55\text{ km/h}$, it gets more efficient.

assessment_items.question_content:
The human eye has different levels of sensitivity for different wavelengths of light. We can measure this sensitivity by comparing it (in percentages) to the highest possible sensitivity.

$S(w)$ models sensitivity as a function of wavelength $w$ (in nanometers).

**Match each statement with the feature of the graph that most closely corresponds to it.**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
The $x$-intercept is the point of intersection between the graph of the function $S(w)$ and the $x$-axis, which is the point on the graph where $S(w)=0$. In this case the horizontal axis represents the variable $w$, but it's common to call that axis the $x$-axis.

This graph has an $x$-intercept at about $(320,0)$, which means that *a wavelength of $\textit{320}$ nanometers is virtually undetectable by the human eye.*



[[☃ image 1]]

A relative maximum (or minimum) point is a point that is higher (or lower) than all of the points surrounding it.

This graph has a relative maximum point at $w=560$, which means that *the human eye is most sensitive to a wavelength of $\textit{560}$ nanometers.*



[[☃ image 1]]

An increasing (or decreasing) interval is a domain interval over which the function values increase (or decrease) as the input variable increases.

In this graph, the interval $[560,800]$ is a decreasing interval. This means that *as the wavelength grows beyond $\textit{560}$ nanometers, the human eye becomes less sensitive to it.*



[[☃ image 1]]

To sum it up, the table should look as follows:

Feature | Statement
:-: | :-:
$x$-intercept | A wavelength of $320$ nanometers is virtually undetectable by the human eye.
Relative maximum or minimum | The human eye is most sensitive to a wavelength of $560$ nanometers.
Increasing or decreasing interval | As the wavelength grows beyond $560$ nanometers, the human eye becomes less sensitive to it.

options.alt: The first quadrant of a coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by forty and is labeled w for the wavelength in nanometers. The y-axis scales by five and is labeled S of w for the sensitivity of a function. The graph of the function is a continuous curve. From left to right, it starts at an x-intercept three hundred twenty, zero and increases through the point four hundred, one, the point four hundred eighty, twenty, and the point five hundred twenty, sixty-five until it reaches a local maximum at five hundred sixty, one hundred. Then it decreases through the point six hundred, sixty-five, the point six hundred forty, twenty, and the point seven hundred twenty, one until it stops at an x-intercept eight hundred, zero.

right: A wavelength of $320$ nanometers is virtually undetectable by the human eye.
The human eye is most sensitive to a wavelength of $560$ nanometers.
As the wavelength grows beyond $560$ nanometers, the human eye becomes less sensitive to it.

assessment_items.question_content:
Vera made a rectangle using a string that is $40\text{ cm}$ long.

$A(w)$ models the area of the rectangle (in $\text{sq. cm}$) as a function of the rectangle's width $w$ (in $\text{cm}$).

**Match each statement with the feature of the graph that most closely corresponds to it.**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
The $x$-intercept is the point of intersection between the graph of the function $A(w)$ and the $w$-axis, which is the point on the graph where $A(w)=0$. In this case the horizontal axis represents the variable $w$, but it's common to call that axis the $x$-axis.

This graph has an $x$-intercept at $(20,0)$, which means that *when the width of the rectangle is $\textit{20 cm}$, it has no area.*



[[☃ image 1]]

A relative maximum (or minimum) point is a point that is higher (or lower) than all of the points surrounding it.

This graph has a relative maximum point where $A(w)=100$, which means that *the greatest possible area of the rectangle is $\textit{100 sq. cm}$.*



[[☃ image 1]]

An increasing (or decreasing) interval is a domain interval over which the function values increase (or decrease) as the input variable increases.

In this graph, the interval $[0,10]$ is an increasing interval. This means that *as the width of the rectangle is extended towards $\textit{10 cm}$, the area of the rectangle grows.*



[[☃ image 1]]

To sum it up, the table should look as follows:

Feature | Statement
:-: | :-:
$x$-intercept | When the width of the rectangle is $20\text{ cm}$, it has no area.
Relative maximum or minimum | The greatest possible area of the rectangle is $100\text{ sq. cm}$.
Increasing or decreasing interval | As the width of the rectangle is extended towards $10\text{ cm}$, the area of the rectangle grows.

options.alt: The first quadrant of a coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by one and is labeled w for the rectangle's width in centimeters. The y-axis scales by five and is labeled A of w for the area of the rectangle. The graph of the function is a continuous curve. From left to right, it starts at zero, zero and increases through the point two, thirty-five, the point four, sixty-five, the point six, eighty-five, and the point eight, ninety until it reaches a local maximum at ten, one hundred. Then it decreases through the point twelve, ninety-five, the point fourteen, eighty-five, the point sixteen, sixty-five, and the point eighteen, thirty-five until it stops at the x-intercept twenty, zero.

right: When the width of the rectangle is $20\text{ cm}$, it has no area.
The greatest possible area of the rectangle is $100\text{ sq. cm}$.
As the width of the rectangle is extended towards $10\text{ cm}$, the area of the rectangle grows.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jan threw a javelin.

$h(d)$ models the height of the tip of the javelin (in meters) as a function of its horizontal distance (in meters) $d$ from the throwing arc.

**Match each statement with the feature of the graph that most closely corresponds to it.**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
The $x$-intercept is the point of intersection between the graph of the function $h(d)$ and the $x$-axis, which is the point on the graph where $h(d)=0$. In this case the horizontal axis represents the variable $d$, but it's common to call that axis the $x$-axis.

This graph has an $x$-intercept at $(90,0)$, which means that *Jan threw the javelin to a distance of $\textit{90}$ meters.*



[[☃ image 1]]

A relative maximum (or minimum) point is a point that is higher (or lower) than all of the points surrounding it.

This graph has a relative maximum point where $h(d)=14$, which means that *the highest point the javelin reached was $\textit{14}$ meters above the ground.*



[[☃ image 1]]

An increasing (or decreasing) interval is a domain interval over which the function values increase (or decrease) as the input variable increases.

In this graph, the interval $[40,90]$ is a decreasing interval. This means that *the javelin began its descent after the $\textit{40}$-meter mark.*



[[☃ image 1]]

To sum it up, the table should look as follows:

Feature | Statement
:-: | :-:
$x$-intercept | Jan threw the javelin to a distance of $90$ meters.
Relative maximum or minimum | The highest point the javelin reached was $14$ meters above the ground.
Increasing or decreasing interval | The javelin began its descent after the $40$-meter mark.

options.alt: The first quadrant of a coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by five and is labeled d for the horizontal distance in meters. The y-axis scales by two and is labeled h of d for the height of the tip of a javelin in meters. The graph of the function is a continuous curve. From left to right, it starts at the y-intercept zero, five and increases through the point ten, nine, the point twenty, twelve, and the point thirty, thirteen until it reaches a local maximum at forty, fourteen. Then it decreases through the point fifty, thirteen, the point sixty, twelve, the point seventy, nine, and the point eighty, five until it stops at the x-intercept ninety, zero.

right: Jan threw the javelin to a distance of $90$ meters.
The highest point the javelin reached was $14$ meters above the ground.
The javelin began its descent after the $40$-meter mark.

assessment_items.question_content:
Pedro went for a very long drive.

$T(d)$ models the temperature (in degrees Celsius) outside Pedro's car as a function of the total distance he drove $d$ (in $\text{km}$).

**Match each statement with the feature of the graph that most closely corresponds to it.**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
A relative maximum (or minimum) point is a point that is higher (or lower) than all of the points surrounding it.

This graph has a relative minimum point where $T(d)=-4$, which means that *the coldest temperature outside the car was $\textit{-4}^\circ\textit{C}$.*



[[☃ image 1]]

A positive (or negative) interval is a domain interval over which the function values are all positive (or negative).

Since $T(d)<0$ over the interval $[110,170]$, this is a negative interval. This means that *between the $\textit{110}^\textit{th}$ and the $\textit{170}^\textit{th}$ kilometers, the outside temperature was below zero.*



[[☃ image 1]]

An increasing (or decreasing) interval is a domain interval over which the function values increase (or decrease) as the input variable increases.

In this graph, the interval $[0,50]$ is an increasing interval. This means that *over the first $\textit{50}\textit{ km}$, the temperature outside the car got warmer.*



[[☃ image 1]]

To sum it up, the table should look as follows:

Feature | Statement
:-: | :-:
Relative maximum or minimum | The coldest temperature outside the car was $-4^\circ\text{C}$.
Positive or negative interval | Between the $110^\text{th}$ and the $170^\text{th}$ kilometers, the outside temperature was below zero.
Increasing or decreasing interval | Over the first $50\text{ km}$, the temperature outside the car got warmer.

options.alt: The first quadrant of a coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by ten and is labeled d for the distance drove in kilometers. The y-axis scales by one and is labeled T of d for the temperature, in degrees Celsius, outside the car. The graph of the function is a continuous curve. From left to right, it starts at the y-intercept zero, six and increases through the point ten, nine, the point twenty, eleven point five, and the point thirty, thirteen until it reaches a local maximum at fifty, sixteen. Then it decreases through the point sixty, fifteen point five, the point seventy, fourteen, the point eighty, fourteen, and the point one hundred, five. It continues decreasing through the x-intercept one hundred ten, zero, the point one hundred twenty, negative one, and the point one hundred forty, negative three point five until a local minimum at one hundred fifty, negative four. Then it increases though the point one hundred sixty, negative three point five and the x-intercept one hundred seventy, zero until it reaches a local maximum at one hundred seventy-eight, three point five. Then is decreases to one hundred eighty-five, three, and increases again to two hundred, six.

right: The coldest temperature outside the car was $-4^\circ\text{C}$.
Between the $110^\text{th}$ and the $170^\text{th}$ kilometers, the outside temperature was below zero.
Over the first $50\text{ km}$, the temperature outside the car got warmer.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jada sells ground paprika.

$P(w)$ models Jada's weekly profit (in dollars, where a negative profit means Jada lost money) as a function of the total weight of paprika $w$ she sold over that week (in kilograms).

**Match each statement with the feature of the graph that most closely corresponds to it.**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
The $y$-intercept is the point of intersection between the graph of the function $P(w)$ and the $y$-axis, which is the point on the graph where $w=0$. 

The $y$-intercept in this graph is $(0,-250)$, which means that *if Jada doesn't sell anything, she loses $\$250$.*



[[☃ image 1]]

A positive (or negative) interval is a domain interval over which the function values are all positive (or negative).

Since $P(w)>0$ over the interval $[35,\infty)$, this is a positive interval. This means that *Jada needs to sell more than $\textit{35}$ kilograms in order to make a profit.*



[[☃ image 1]]

An increasing (or decreasing) interval is a domain interval over which the function values increase (or decrease) as the input variable increases.

This graph is always increasing. This means that *the more paprika Jada sells, the more profit she makes.*



[[☃ image 1]]

To sum it up, the table should look as follows:

Feature | Statement
:-: | :-:
$y$-intercept | If Jada doesn't sell anything, she loses $\$250$.
Positive or negative interval | Jada needs to sell more than $35$ kilograms in order to make a profit.
Increasing or decreasing interval | The more paprika Jada sells, the more profit she makes.

options.alt: The first and fourth quadrants of a coordinate plane. The x-axis scales by five and is labeled w for the weight, in kilograms, of paprika sold over the week. The y-axis scales by fifty and is labeled P of w for Jada's weekly profit in dollars. The graph of the function is a straight line. It starts at the y-intercept zero, negative two hundred fifty and increases through the x-intercept thirty-five, zero and goes through the point one hundred, five hundred.

right: If Jada doesn't sell anything, she loses $\$250$.
Jada needs to sell more than $35$ kilograms in order to make a profit.
The more paprika Jada sells, the more profit she makes.
